pattern: Fill a small grid one cell at a time with introverts, extroverts, or empty seats, where seating someone next to an already-seated neighbor changes both people's happiness at once, and search for the arrangement that maximizes total happiness.
hints: Placing a person changes the happiness of that person and of every neighbor already seated directly above or to its left, so process cells in reading order and only ever look backward.
Only the previous row and the current row's filled-so-far cells can still affect future decisions, so compress the last n placements into a small base-3 fingerprint instead of storing the whole grid.

Memoize on (cell index, introverts left to place, extroverts left to place, recent-placements fingerprint) and at each cell try leaving it empty, seating an introvert, or seating an extrovert, keeping whichever total is best.

edge_inputs.description: nobody is placed when both counts are zero
a single row forces every neighbor relationship to be horizontal
a single column forces every neighbor relationship to be vertical
only introverts are available, so the optimum still spreads them apart rather than clustering
every cell of a small grid is filled by a mix of both types

stress.description: small random grids with a random introvert/extrovert split up to the seat count
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, 3)
    n = rng.randint(1, 3)
    cells = m * n
    total_people = rng.randint(0, min(cells, 6))
    intro = rng.randint(0, total_people)
    extro = total_people - intro
    return [m, n, intro, extro]
stress.note: grid capped at 3x3 so an exhaustive-search oracle stays fast
